context:
## Dieses Dokument

- Titel: Keep out of Politics!
- Signatur: SZ-AAP/W-AA103.11
- Datum: 1938
- Sprache: Englisch
- Objekttyp: Zeitungsausschnitt
- Umfang: 1 Blatt
- Schreibinstrument: Violette
                                       Tinte
- Hand: Lotte Zweig

pages.transcription:
Query Book 2, 1938

Jews in Western Civilisation

KEEP OUT OF POLITICS!

by Stefan Zweig

THE Jews find themselves to-day in one of the most difficult
periods of their history, especially of modern times. But it
is not that which should be discussed, but rather the exertion
of all our powers in order to overcome this phase by moral
means, both in our own lives and in relation to the outside
world. At bottom, each individual—albeit with an added
sense of responsibility—must decide for himself how he is
to act. But there are certain lines of conduct which should
be observed by all.

It is essential to speak out clearly and honestly, though
some ambitious people will passionately dispute what I have
to say. There is nothing that has so promoted the anti-
Semitic movement as the fact that Jews have been too much
in evidence in various countries in various trends of political
life, and in opposing parties, too often as leaders. It is not
an accident that the Hitler movement began in Munich,
where the revolution was led by Jews (Eisner, Levine, Lan-
dauer, etc., etc.), most of them people who were not born
in Bavaria, and had no real inner relationship with the
country, and were constantly quarrelling among themselves
about their methods and activities. There was nothing more
natural than that the reaction against the failures of that
revolution should have been exploited for the purpose of
agitating against Jews, with the result that anti-Semitism
immediately became popular in Bavaria. In politics, every
point of vantage offered by the enemy must be exploited,
and the Jewish leader of a party presents an irresistible target.
They may not know it, they certainly would not wish it,
but Jewish leaders imperil the cause they desire to promote.
They become a danger to that party in the State to which
they belong, and at the same time they are a danger to the
Jews, on whom they impose a burden of responsibility for
their actions which they have no wish to bear.

Should a Jew, then, take no part in political life? some
people will ask with indignation. Is the Jew to resign his
citizenship rights? Is he alone to be denied the right to be
active in the political or social movements of the country of
which he is a citizen? Not at all! I have not said that.
But I do think it is desirable that Jews should not be leaders
of political parties, with responsibility for taking fateful
decisions, even when they have the capacity and the vocation
for it. There is nothing that proves one’s loyalty to a party
or an idea more than the ability to sacrifice oneself for
its sake, to have the capacity to occupy the first, the most
prominent position, and to place oneself voluntarily in the
ranks, and to serve there with two-fold zeal. The example
of Benjamin Disraeli, which some may bring up against me,
seems to my mind no longer applicable to our time. We
are living in the most severe social and political crisis of
modern times, and irritation has become latent and danger-
ous in vast circles. The masses feel that their standard of
living is being cut down, they see their security imperilled,
and it is utterly impossible to make them understand the
real causes of the world crisis. It is useless to talk political
economy to them. They want to see visible, tangible causes
of the crisis, and the example of Germany shows (as history
does throughout the ages) that in a crisis it is easy to whip
up enmity or at least to sow distrust against a single group,
and to make that group appear solely responsible for all
the evils. Time and again the Jews have served as the
scapegoat, and we see in Germany now that the anti-
Jewish cry is still powerful enough to make it seem that
even a lost war and a severe economic crisis is their fault
and only their fault. Anti-Semitism is still a welcome tool
in a time of crisis, and political parties or groups who wish
to exploit it have their task made much easier for them if
they can point to Jews who are the leaders of political or
economic groups, and ascribe to them the sole responsibility
for the failure of military, political, or economic operations.
Any responsibility nowadays, as things are going, is of seven-
fold magnitude, and for a Jew seventy times seven. It is
therefore a painful experience for Jews to see a Jew taking
upon himself now decisions that affect the lives of millions
of people. For we must all bear a part of the hazardous
burden of every possible error, must share the responsibility
for every action of every Jew who out of ambition assumes
such responsibility.

Certainly, so far as rights go, every individual Jew has
the same right as any other citizen to hold the highest
position in the State; but in reality, as things are now, only
his right is the same, his responsibility is immeasurably
greater. That is why we must come out against those few
ambitious persons, no matter to what party they belong,
who refuse to make the sacrifice of working quietly in the
ranks, and unobtrusively serving their party or their idea,
instead of thrusting themselves into the lightlight. There
are countless ways of serving an idea—books, speech, self-
sacrificing, devoted work.

But it seems to me a mistake at such of stormy
passage, on a voyage of such uncertain direction, to insist on
having the captain’s post. It seems to me false vanity for
Jews to rejoice if one of them to-day holds an exalted, com-
manding, deciding position in public political life. We
should rather deplore the fact that these few individuals do
not exercise the tact and restraint that are for Jews at all
times, and more especially to-day, the imperative command,
and the innermost wisdom. It is not by pushing forward,
but by consciously holding back that a man reveals his
moral strength. It is not the agitators, the zealots, and the
politicians who are our true spiritual expression, but those
who in a thousand different ways re-create the idea of the
invisible God that we have brought into the world, and
which does not belong to any Party or to any class, but to
the whole of mankind.

77
pages.notes: Druckerzeugnis (Zeitschriftenartikel). Handschriftliche Annotation oben links: "Query Book 2, 1938" in violetter Tinte (Lotte Zweig).

pages.transcription:
Jews in Western Civilisation
A DIALOGUE ON RADICALISM—(Continued from page 76)

paintings of a Marc Chagall, a Pissarro, Modigliani, Kandinsky, or Leo Bakst, or the German Jews Meidner and Fein-
inger; or, one generation ago, the works of such "new"
artists as the great Dutch Jew Josef Israels and the German
Max Liebermann. Look at the skyscrapers of the Anglo-
Jewish architects Delissa, and Ernest and Charles Joseph, of
the German Eric Mendelssohn, now also in England!

X: If you wish to describe a joy of progressive art as
"revolutionary," you are right. But there is a great difference between mentally and spiritually conditioned
radicalism, and a sort of speculation with the aim of "épater
le bourgeois." On the other hand, the Jews have given to
the world other, shall we say more classical, artists, such as
Anatole Raphael Mengs, Solomon Hart, or the great Solomon
J. Solomon, R.A., Sir William Rothenstein, or the French
animal-painter Rosa Bonheur. Belgrave Square, as well as
the London club quarter, includes many buildings designed
by Disraeli’s cousin, George Bassevi.

I: I grant you that the term "revolutionary," as
applied to science and art, holds a rather favourable meaning. It is mostly foreign Jews, though, who import bold
innovations, while their English co-religionists tend to be
eclectic and traditionalist. This fact goes to prove to some
extent your theory of oppression breeding radicalism. The
Russians Meyerhold and Tairoff, the German Max Reinhardt,
had a revolutionising effect on the stage, whereas our representative Jews like, say, Augustus Harris or Arthur Collins,
sought to present us with the best of the traditional. The
French Jewesses Rachel and Sarah Bernhardt, the Italian
Moissi, and the Austrian Elizabeth Bergner created new
theatrical styles of their own; our Lily Hanbury, Julia Neilson, Ann and Austin Trevor, and many more, emulated
rather than invented. Even on the screen a Polish Jew, Paul
Muni, stands out by virtue of his independent characterisation, though the film industry seems to embrace far less
Jewish names than I expected, after the oft-repeated accusation of its being "Jew-ridden."

X: Your observations are correct, and I can further
supplement them: In the realm of music, the German Jews
Gustav Mahler and Arnold Schoenberg, the Frenchman
Darius Milhaud, were "revolutionising" factors, while English Sir Frederic Cowen earned his just reputation on already
trodden, classical paths. I am not sure whether it may be
permitted to call composers of a past age "innovators"
—I am referring to Jews like Mendelssohn-Bartholdy,
Meyerbeer, Jacques Offenbach, Halévy, Saint-Saëns, and
Goldmark—for despite their creative originality they perhaps
testified to the intimate connection between the highest Jewish
achievement and that of the people adopted by the creators.
This is certainly true of practising musicians, such as Rubinstein, Joachim, Huberman, Kreisler, Yehudi Menuhin, Mischa
Elman, Mark Hambourg, Harriet Cohen, and the famous
conductors Bruno Walter, Sir Julius Benedict, Sir Landon
Ronald.

I: Perhaps that effect is produced by music only, with
its too-stern discipline. Jewish radicalism in literature
cannot be disputed: from the earliest emancipated German
Jews Heinrich Heine, Boerne, down to Maximilian Harden,
Franz Werfel, and Stefan Zweig—audacious subject-matter is
even more prominent than perfection of form. The works
of Franco-Jewish writers, such as Marcel Proust, Tristan
Bernard, Henri Bernstein, may be similarly classified. In
search for English-writing examples one is forced to point
at American "muck-rakers" like G. J. Nathan, or, at most,
our own sometimes irreverent Siegfried Sassoon. Our Israel
Zangwill, Leonard Merrick, G. B. Stern, Sarah Gertrude
Millin are far less clearly outlined personalities, in spite of
their admitted importance.

X: I will instance the field of philosophy now for
further proof of Jewish "radicalism"—for is not the
philosopher’s task the covering of new ground in wisdom
and knowledge? From Spinoza—expelled and persecuted
for his breach with tradition by blind Jewish orthodoxy,
Moses Mendelssohn, who gave Western thought unto his
brethren, down to the Germans Mauthner and Otto Weininger, the French Jews H. Bergson, the sociologist E. Durckheim, and C. Lévy-Bruhl, the Englishmen E. Alexander and
A. Wolf—every one of these broke with established doctrines; even though one cannot call them revolutionists in a
political sense, as one does the ideological pillars of Soviet
Communism, of whom Trotsky, Zinoviev, and Lunacharsky were Jews. This brings us to the next question: Can
progressive science as much as exist without radicalism? Is
evolution, that is, mere further development continued in a
fixed direction, sufficient—or is not complete re-formation
of thought required, in other words, revolution?

I: Radicalism has an undoubted directly productive
effect on Pure Science, and it is constructive even while
overthrowing established teachings. The inquisitive, explorer, ever-dissatisfied inquiring mind, as really or supposedly
typified by the Jewish intellectual, seems therefore destined
to achieve the greatest glory in this particular field. That is
how I explain the uncommonly high percentage of Jews or
those descended from Jews among the winners of the greatest
scientific awards, such as the Nobel Prize, bestowed by an
exclusively Gentile committee. The theory is further supported by the fact that exact science—i.e. physics, mathematics, chemistry—shows the highest percentage of Jewish
prize-winners.

X: What does this mean? The fact that most of them
emerged from German culture—more than a third of the
German and Austrian Nobel Prize-winners were Jews,
a tenth in relation to the world—seems to me a solid argument for the interaction between various nations and
their Jews, and an argument against defined, specifically
Jewish contributions—so the Jews of other countries,
without a German background, cannot boast such numerous
and unsurpassed feats. If you can use the term "radicalism"
in the field of science, it is only with reference to a man like
Streicher, who urges the prohibition of medicaments invented
by Jewish doctors and chemists. The German Nobel
Prize-substitute, for which only "Aryans" are eligible, was
founded after the last Nobel Peace Prize came to the pacifist
Carl von Ossietzky when he lay dying in a concentration
camp. This "made-in-Germany"-edition will have difficulty in collecting a winners’ list of equal distinction . . .
78
pages.notes: Druckerzeugnis (Zeitschriftenartikel). Fortsetzung des Artikels auf der Rückseite.
Farbskala/Grauskala fuer Archivierung. Überlappung des Textes von Seite 2 (Ausschnitt).

result.confidence_notes: Der Text ist sehr gut lesbar, da es sich um einen sauberen Druck handelt. Die handschriftliche Annotation auf Seite 1 ist ebenfalls klar lesbar.
